summary: Iteration 25: Multi-Metric Combined Anomaly Detection (Phase 3) - All tests passed. Backend: 19/19 pytest tests passed. Frontend: All UI elements verified. Combined anomaly rule exists with correct weights (battery=0.7, signal=0.3, trigger_threshold=60, correlation_bonus=10). Fleet simulation triggers multi_metric anomalies. Fixed bug: added combined_anomaly to KNOWN_RULE_NAMES in rule.py schema.

backend.test_health_rules_endpoint_returns_200: PASS - Operator can access health-rules endpoint
backend.test_combined_anomaly_rule_exists: PASS - combined_anomaly rule in rules list
backend.test_combined_anomaly_rule_has_correct_t: PASS - Has weight_battery=0.7, weight_signal=0.3, trigger_threshold=60, correlation_bonus=10
backend.test_combined_anomaly_rule_can_be_toggle: PASS - Toggle on/off works after schema fix
backend.test_fleet_simulation_triggers_anomalies: PASS - Aggressive battery drain triggers anomalies
backend.test_fleet_simulation_includes_multi_met: PASS - anomaly_details includes multi_metric with scores
backend.test_multi_metric_anomalies_have_correct: PASS - reason_json has type, battery_score, signal_score, combined_score, weights, correlation_flag, correlation_bonus, trigger_threshold
backend.test_signal_strength_anomalies_still_exi: PASS - Signal anomalies exist alongside multi_metric

frontend.multi_metric_combined_intelligence_secti: PASS - Section with Zap icon visible
frontend.green_banner_no_combined_anomalies: PASS - Shows 'No combined anomalies detected. Devices are within acceptable multi-metric thresholds.'
frontend.battery_anomaly_section: PASS - Battery Anomaly Intelligence section visible
frontend.signal_anomaly_section: PASS - Signal Strength Anomaly Intelligence section visible

critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Added combined_anomaly to KNOWN_RULE_NAMES in /app/backend/app/schemas/rule.py - was missing which prevented toggle/update operations
Combined anomaly detection correctly implements 3-layer scoring: (1) weighted sum, (2) correlation bonus, (3) threshold check
Individual battery_slope and signal_strength anomalies are NOT suppressed by combined detection - working as designed
10-minute cooldown correctly prevents duplicate multi_metric anomalies
Fleet simulation correctly triggers all 3 detection types: battery_slope, signal_strength, multi_metric
UI correctly displays Multi-Metric Combined Intelligence section with Zap icon (purple)
Green banner shows when no combined anomalies exist; anomaly cards would show battery_score, signal_score, weights, correlation badge, combined_score
seed_data_creation: Created multi_metric anomalies via fleet simulation with aggressive battery drain (battery_score=100, combined_score=70 > threshold 60). All simulated anomalies are correctly excluded from default endpoint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-Metric Combined Anomaly Detection (Phase 3) fully tested. Key implementation: (1) _detect_combined_anomalies in baseline_scheduler.py computes weighted combined score from recent battery_slope + signal_strength anomalies, (2) combined_score = battery × 0.7 + signal × 0.3 + correlation_bonus (if both active), (3) Creates multi_metric anomaly if score > 60, (4) UI shows 'Multi-Metric Combined Intelligence' section with Zap icon. Note: combined_anomaly rule now in KNOWN_RULE_NAMES after fix.
